accepted.reason: Gini impurity, information gain, and split logic are compact tree-learning kernels with clear arithmetic/branching structure.
Opcode interpreter loop with parameter modes and control-flow jumps is a useful state-machine optimization target.
Soil temperature damping-depth update is a compact environmental simulation kernel with repeated nonlinear transforms.
Gamma-parameter transforms and vectorized parameter updates are compact math kernels with repeated exponent/log operations.
Histogram bucket index computation and streaming aggregate updates are practical low-level counting kernels.

skipped.reason: Tightly coupled to project-specific C interfaces and assets; not a standalone kernel.
Depends on external breakage operator implementation and scientific stack specifics, limiting portability.
Application/linking object graph code is framework-heavy and not representative numeric optimization data.
Low-level extension glue and object model handling are not ideal for reusable standalone GRPO examples.
Large geometry/prototyping code appears experimental and partially inconsistent for clean training extraction.
